steps.title: Treat the name as essential
steps.content: The phrase "plant cell biologist" gives Lee's role, and "Yuree Lee" identifies the specific scientist who showed the result. Because the name is needed to identify the author, it should not be set off as parenthetical information.

steps.title: Remove unnecessary commas
steps.content: Choice A inserts a comma before the name. Choice B inserts a comma after the name. Choice D uses commas around the name, wrongly treating it as nonessential. The sentence needs no comma between the title and the identifying name. The answer is C.
